notes: Original Cortlandt Street station was destroyed on September 11, 2001. Rebuilt and reopened as WTC Cortlandt on September 8, 2018 with full ADA compliance. The rebuilt station features extensive modern safety and accessibility systems not present in the original 1918 station.

New station opened March 16, 2009, replacing the old 1905 loop station that could not accommodate full-length trains. Built ADA-accessible from day one with $530 million in federal 9/11 recovery funding. Severely damaged by Hurricane Sandy on October 29, 2012; rebuilt and reopened June 27, 2017. The 2009 date is used as the upgrade year since that is when the station first became accessible.
